scenarios.scenario: A bounded model repair stays draft until the original checkpoint is verified and a person promotes it
An unrepairable path remains not tested and cannot be promoted

visualEvidence.rationale: RUN-04 changes model-operated browser repair, deterministic verification, promotion, and unresolved recovery behavior. Source-bound deterministic runs therefore retain before/after screenshots, machine-readable receipts, independent-recorder reports, and sanitized Playwright traces. No product screen changed, so a narrow-viewport duplicate is not applicable; the execution boundary is proven at the controlled 1280 by 900 viewport. Live GPT-5.6 Sol assets remain pending and are not represented by these deterministic-adapter captures.

assets.altText: Desktop capture of the controlled interface-drift fixture at the obsolete student route, showing that the student checkpoint moved to the learner route.
assets.caption: The frozen version-one replay starts in a visible drift state before any repair discovery.

assets.altText: Desktop capture after deterministic model-adapter discovery followed the reviewed learner link and submit control, showing the green fictional completion receipt.
assets.caption: Discovery exercised only the reviewed navigation and submit controls inside the exact fictional origin.

assets.altText: Desktop capture from a fresh deterministic browser showing the same fictional completion receipt after the bounded candidate replay.
assets.caption: A zero-model replay independently reached the original submission checkpoint before human promotion.

assets.altText: Machine-readable deterministic model-adapter run with three executed actions, reviewed NAVIGATE and SUBMIT controls, completion observed, and zero policy violations.
assets.caption: The discovery loop completed only after deterministic browser state, not model text, established completion.

assets.altText: Independent recorder report for bounded discovery with reviewed browser actions and visible fictional submission evidence.
assets.caption: The recorder observed discovery effects separately from the model-adapter action loop.

assets.altText: Machine-readable BOUNDED_DRAFT changing only the relative student path and reviewed submit selector while preserving frozen authority and checkpoints.
assets.caption: The model-adapter output remains a non-active bounded draft after discovery.

assets.altText: Fresh deterministic candidate outcome marked COMPLETED with zero verification-model invocations and the original submission checkpoint verified.
assets.caption: The candidate passed the frozen checkpoint in a separate zero-model browser session.

assets.altText: Independent recorder report from the fresh deterministic verification browser with the required fictional submission checkpoint visible.
assets.caption: Recorder visibility is required in addition to the replay adapter's checkpoint result.

assets.altText: Machine-readable VERIFIED_DRAFT record binding the exact repair hash, source replay, verified frozen checkpoint, and visible recorder state.
assets.caption: Deterministic automation verifies the draft but does not activate it.

assets.altText: Machine-readable promotion receipt naming a human reviewer and binding the verified repair to deterministic replay version two.
assets.caption: Only the named fictional human reviewer appends the promoted replay version.

assets.altText: Sanitized Playwright trace of the source replay stopping at the moved student route.
assets.caption: The source trace preserves the pre-repair drift boundary with no credential patterns.

assets.altText: Sanitized Playwright trace of reviewed-control discovery through the moved learner route and fictional submission.
assets.caption: The discovery trace is paired with independent recorder evidence and a zero-credential sanitizer report.

assets.altText: Sanitized Playwright trace of the fresh zero-model candidate replay verifying the original checkpoint.
assets.caption: The verification trace is separate from model discovery and contains no repository path or credential pattern.

assets.altText: Desktop capture of the controlled failure fixture showing the fictional submission service unavailable and no request dispatched.
assets.caption: The unresolved branch starts from a visible controlled service failure.

assets.altText: Desktop capture after bounded discovery attempted the reviewed submit control, still showing service unavailable and no request dispatched.
assets.caption: The model-adapter cannot turn an unavailable service into a passing repair.

assets.altText: Machine-readable deterministic model-adapter run marked FAILED and MODEL_STOPPED_BEFORE_COMPLETION with no completion observed.
assets.caption: The bounded attempt records failure instead of manufacturing a passing result.

assets.altText: Independent recorder report for the unresolved service failure with no verified submission checkpoint.
assets.caption: The recorder independently confirms that the protected effect was not observed.

assets.altText: Machine-readable repair record marked UNRESOLVED with no candidate and a NO_CANDIDATE violation.
assets.caption: An unavailable service remains unresolved rather than becoming a selector or path repair.

assets.altText: Machine-readable verification marked NOT_TESTED and REPAIR_NOT_BOUNDED with no verified checkpoint and recorder visibility not tested.
assets.caption: The unresolved proposal cannot be verified or promoted.

assets.altText: Sanitized Playwright trace of the source replay encountering the controlled unavailable submission service.
assets.caption: The failure source trace preserves the missing-checkpoint state with no credential patterns.

assets.altText: Sanitized Playwright trace of bounded discovery stopping without completion in the controlled failure fixture.
assets.caption: The failure discovery trace shows no verified checkpoint and no credential patterns.

proofs.description: Bounded draft assessment, exact checkpoint verification, human-only promotion, and append-only in-memory and PostgreSQL history
Database-enforced model provenance, frozen repair scope, deterministic verification, human promotion, and immutable history
Executed trusted-control observation and deterministic path/selector candidate derivation
Bounded diff, scope-review, missing-checkpoint, and named-human promotion examples
Executed control-to-replay candidate contract and ambiguity rejection
Task-scoped RUN-04 trace sanitization with verification-root containment
Seeded PROP-05 authorization, operation, binding, checkpoint, and promotion invariants
Real isolated-browser drift discovery, fresh deterministic checkpoint verification, recorder visibility, and unresolved path
Append-only repair draft, verification, replay, and promotion history in memory and PostgreSQL
Successful bounded repair and unresolved-not-tested recovery stories
Isolated source drift, model discovery, shared-recorder verification, human promotion, and evidence capture bindings
Required opt-in GPT-5.6 Sol repair against the seeded interface drift with deterministic verification
No-main opt-in live repair workflow with encrypted key and explicit sanitized upload allowlist
Task-scoped verification-root trace sanitizer with repository-path replacement and credential-pattern rejection
Zero-credential sanitizer report for the drifted source replay trace
Zero-credential sanitizer report for bounded discovery
Zero-credential sanitizer report for fresh deterministic candidate verification
Zero-credential sanitizer report for the unavailable source replay
Zero-credential sanitizer report for the unresolved discovery attempt
Frozen version-one replay outcome proving the initial interface drift
Executed reviewed controls from which the bounded path and selector candidate was deterministically derived
Human-authored version-two replay produced only after exact deterministic verification
Frozen source replay outcome proving the controlled service failure did not satisfy the checkpoint
Bounded executed-control observations that yielded no repair candidate for the service outage
Current deterministic proof, pending live and source-bound evidence gates, and bounded claim

findings: Five deterministic Playwright traces scanned three text streams each; repository-root occurrences were replaced and all credential-pattern counts were zero.
Five screenshots were visually inspected and contain only controlled fixture content and reserved .invalid identities.
Curated JSON and expanded sanitized traces contain no local user path, OpenAI key name, API-key pattern, private-key marker, or configured fictional secret value.

knownLimitations: The required source-bound live GPT-5.6 Sol repair has not run yet, so RUN-04 is not complete.

GitHub Actions runs 29825459582 and 29825515860 were triggered at source commit 0082dcceece05db5986b43fb5b4dc3ecd69072e6, but GitHub refused to start every job because the account payment or Actions spending limit requires attention; those pre-execution failures are not product evidence.
Current deterministic coverage proves one seeded relative-path and selector change plus one outage; it does not establish repair effectiveness on arbitrary products.
A verified repair remains a draft until a named human appends the deterministic replay version; scope-changing proposals never enter the same replay lineage.
A verified named test is not evidence of safety, compliance, or behavior outside the frozen checkpoint scope.
